### `tui/src/chatwidget/settings_popups.rs`

`orchestration` · `interactive popup opening during command/UI actions`

This file contains the UI-entry methods that open several settings-adjacent popups from `ChatWidget`. The theme picker gathers contextual inputs — current theme override, Codex home directory, and last rendered terminal width converted to `u16` — and passes them into `theme_picker::build_theme_picker_params` before showing the resulting selection view in the bottom pane.

Personality selection has more policy. The public entrypoint first blocks use before session startup completes, then checks whether the current effective model supports personalities. If either condition fails, it emits an informational or error history message instead of opening the popup. The actual popup builder constructs two selectable personalities (`Friendly` and `Pragmatic`), marks the current one, and attaches a closure per item that sends three app events: override the turn context, update the widget personality, and persist the selection. The popup also includes a small header and standard footer hint.

The experimental popup scans the global `FEATURES` registry, keeping only specs whose stage exposes an experimental-menu name and description, then wraps those into an `ExperimentalFeaturesView` with the widget’s event sender and list keymap. Two small helpers map `Personality` enum values to stable labels and descriptions used by the popup.

#### Function details

##### `ChatWidget::open_theme_picker`  (lines 9–21)

```
fn open_theme_picker(&mut self)
```

**Purpose**: Builds and opens the theme picker popup using the current theme override, Codex home directory, and terminal width when available. It is the widget-side entrypoint for theme selection UI.

**Data flow**: It reads `self.config.tui_theme`, attempts to discover Codex home with `find_codex_home().ok()`, converts `last_rendered_width` to `Option<u16>`, builds picker params with `crate::theme_picker::build_theme_picker_params(...)`, and passes those params to `bottom_pane.show_selection_view`. It returns nothing.

**Call relations**: This method is invoked by higher-level command/UI flows when the user wants to change themes. It delegates parameter construction to the theme-picker module and rendering to the bottom pane.

*Call graph*: calls 1 internal fn (build_theme_picker_params); 1 external calls (find_codex_home).


##### `ChatWidget::open_personality_popup`  (lines 23–39)

```
fn open_personality_popup(&mut self)
```

**Purpose**: Validates that personality selection is currently allowed, then opens the personality picker for the active model. It emits user-facing messages instead of opening the popup when startup is incomplete or the model lacks personality support.

**Data flow**: It reads session-configured state via `is_session_configured()` and model capability via `current_model_supports_personality()`. On failure it writes an info or error message to history; on success it calls `open_personality_popup_for_current_model()`. It returns nothing.

**Call relations**: This is the guarded public entrypoint for personality selection. It delegates actual popup construction to `ChatWidget::open_personality_popup_for_current_model` only after passing startup and model-capability checks.

*Call graph*: calls 1 internal fn (open_personality_popup_for_current_model); 1 external calls (format!).


##### `ChatWidget::open_personality_popup_for_current_model`  (lines 41–91)

```
fn open_personality_popup_for_current_model(&mut self)
```

### `tui/src/bottom_pane/skills_toggle_view.rs`

`domain_logic` · `request handling`

This file defines `SkillsToggleView`, a self-contained bottom-pane view for managing skill enablement. Each `SkillsToggleItem` carries the visible name, canonical skill name, description, current enabled flag, and backing config path. The view stores the full item list, a `ScrollState`, completion flag, event sender, a rendered header, a footer hint line derived from the active `ListKeymap`, the current search query, and the filtered visible index list.

Construction builds a two-line header, initializes state, computes the footer hint, and immediately applies filtering. `apply_filter` is the core state transition: it preserves the previously selected underlying item when possible, either shows all items for an empty query or fuzzy-matches via `match_skill`, sorts matches by score then display name, rebuilds `filtered_indices`, restores or defaults selection, and ensures the selected row is visible. `build_rows` then turns the filtered items into `GenericDisplayRow`s with a leading `›` marker on the selected row and `[x]` / `[ ]` enabled markers embedded in the row name.

Keyboard handling distinguishes plain text entry from navigation aliases so typing letters feeds search instead of accidentally moving. Backspace edits the query, space or accept toggles the selected item and emits `AppEvent::SetSkillEnabled`, cancel closes the view, and close also emits `ManageSkillsClosed` plus a forced skill list refresh. Rendering draws a styled block, header, two-line search prompt, compact single-line list, and dimmed footer hint.

#### Function details

##### `SkillsToggleView::new`  (lines 60–84)

```
fn new(
        items: Vec<SkillsToggleItem>,
        app_event_tx: AppEventSender,
        keymap: ListKeymap,
    ) -> Self
```

**Purpose**: Constructs the manage-skills popup with header text, keymap-derived footer hint, empty search state, and an initial filtered view.

**Data flow**: Consumes the item list, `AppEventSender`, and `ListKeymap`. It builds a `ColumnRenderable` header with bold title and dim subtitle lines, initializes `ScrollState::new()`, stores the sender and keymap, computes `footer_hint` with `skills_toggle_hint_line`, initializes empty `search_query` and `filtered_indices`, then calls `apply_filter()` before returning the view.

**Call relations**: This constructor is used when the manage-skills popup is opened. It immediately delegates to `apply_filter` so selection and visible rows are valid from the first render.

*Call graph*: calls 3 internal fn (new, skills_toggle_hint_line, new); called by 3 (footer_hint_uses_list_keymap_accept_and_cancel, renders_basic_popup, open_manage_skills_popup); 4 external calls (new, from, new, new).


**Purpose**: Returns the number of currently visible rows after filtering.

**Data flow**: Reads `self.filtered_indices.len()` and returns it as `usize`.

**Call relations**: Navigation helpers use this to compute movement bounds against the filtered list rather than the full item list.

*Call graph*: called by 6 (jump_bottom, jump_top, move_down, move_up, page_down, page_up).


##### `SkillsToggleView::max_visible_rows`  (lines 90–92)

```
fn max_visible_rows(len: usize) -> usize
```

**Purpose**: Computes the viewport size to use for scrolling, capped by `MAX_POPUP_ROWS` but never below one.

**Data flow**: Takes a row count `len`, computes `len.max(1)`, then returns `MAX_POPUP_ROWS.min(...)`.

**Call relations**: Filtering and navigation helpers use this to keep `ScrollState::ensure_visible` and page movement aligned with the rendered list height.


##### `SkillsToggleView::apply_filter`  (lines 94–137)

```
fn apply_filter(&mut self)
```

**Purpose**: Rebuilds the filtered visible item list from the current search query while preserving selection on the same underlying item when possible.

**Data flow**: Reads the current selected visible index and maps it through `filtered_indices` to an underlying item index. It trims `search_query`; for an empty filter it fills `filtered_indices` with all item indices, otherwise it fuzzy-matches each item via `match_skill(filter, display_name, skill_name)`, collects `(index, score)` pairs, sorts by score then item name, and stores only the indices. It then restores selection to the same underlying item if still present, otherwise selects the first row when any rows remain, clamps selection with `state.clamp_selection(len)`, and updates scroll with `state.ensure_visible(len, visible)`.

**Call relations**: This is the central state-update routine called from `handle_key_event` whenever the search query changes. It depends on `ScrollState` helpers to normalize selection and scrolling after rebuilding the visible list.

*Call graph*: calls 3 internal fn (clamp_selection, ensure_visible, match_skill); called by 1 (handle_key_event); 2 external calls (max_visible_rows, new).


**Purpose**: Converts the filtered item set into render-ready rows showing selection and enabled-state markers.

**Data flow**: Iterates over `filtered_indices` with visible indices, looks up each `SkillsToggleItem`, computes whether that visible row is selected, chooses a leading `›` or space prefix, chooses `x` or space for the enabled marker, truncates the display name with `truncate_skill_name`, formats a combined row name like `› [x] Repo Scout`, copies the description, and returns the resulting `Vec<GenericDisplayRow>`.

**Call relations**: Both `desired_height` and `render` call this so sizing and drawing are based on the same visible row content.


**Purpose**: Processes keyboard input for navigation, search editing, toggling, and closing the manage-skills popup.

It then matches movement bindings (`move_up`, `move_down`, `page_up`, `page_down`, `jump_top`, `jump_bottom`), backspace to delete from `search_query` and `apply_filter`, plain space or accept to `toggle_selected`, cancel to `on_ctrl_c`, and other printable non-control/non-alt characters to append to `search_query` and `apply_filter`. Unrecognized keys are ignored.

**Call relations**: This is the main event handler required by `BottomPaneView`. It orchestrates all local state transitions by delegating to the movement helpers, `apply_filter`, `toggle_selected`, and `on_ctrl_c`.

### `tui/src/bottom_pane/memories_settings_view.rs`

`domain_logic` · `interactive settings popup`

This file defines a compact settings popup around three menu items: two boolean settings (`Use memories` and `Generate memories`) and one destructive action (`Reset all memories`). `MemoriesSettingsView` stores those items, a primary `ScrollState`, an optional secondary `ScrollState` for the reset-confirmation subview, a completion flag, an `AppEventSender`, a prebuilt documentation link line, and the list keymap.

The view has two modes. In normal settings mode, rows are built from `MemoriesMenuItem`, with setting rows rendered as `› [x] Name` or `  [ ] Name` and the action row rendered without a checkbox. In reset-confirmation mode, the list is replaced by exactly two rows: `Reset all memories` and `Go back`, and a different header is shown. `active_state`/`active_state_mut` abstract over which `ScrollState` is currently driving selection and scrolling.

Keyboard handling mirrors other list popups: movement keys update the active scroll state, space toggles only setting rows in normal mode, Enter calls `save`, and cancel/Ctrl+C call `cancel`. `save` is mode-sensitive: in confirmation mode it either emits `AppEvent::ResetMemories` and closes or returns to the settings list; in normal mode it either opens the confirmation subview when the reset action is selected or emits `AppEvent::UpdateMemorySettings { use_memories, generate_memories }` and closes. Rendering uses the shared popup surface styling, shared row measurement/rendering helpers, and shows a hyperlink-marked docs URL only in normal settings mode.

#### Function details

**Call relations**: Used when the memories popup is opened.

### `tui/src/bottom_pane/hooks_browser_view.rs`

`domain_logic` · `interactive popup handling`

This file defines `HooksBrowserView`, a stateful popup over `HooksListEntry`. The view stores the sorted hook metadata, the current page (`Events` or `Handlers(HookEventName)`), shared `ScrollState`, completion flag, event sender, and list keymap. Construction sorts hooks by `display_order`, starts on the event-summary page, and preselects the first event row that has any hooks needing review, falling back to the first event.

The event page aggregates all protocol hook events using `HookEventName::iter()`. For each event it computes installed count, active count, and review-needed count; active means `enabled` plus trusted/managed status, so untrusted enabled hooks are intentionally not counted as active. The page can also prepend a warning banner when any hooks need review and an `Issues` section built from `entry.warnings` and `entry.errors`.

Opening an event switches to the handlers page for that event, resets scroll state, and selects the first handler. Handler rows show `[x]`, `[ ]`, or `[!]` markers, append `· new` or `· modified` for untrusted/modified hooks, dim managed hooks, and use yellow styling for review-needed hooks. The detail pane below the list shows event, matcher, source, command (wrapped and capped to three lines), timeout, and trust label.

Interaction is page-sensitive. Enter on the event page drills in; Enter or space on the handlers page toggles unmanaged trusted hooks; `t` trusts either the selected review-needed hook or all review-needed hooks on the event page; Esc closes from the event page or returns to the event page from a handler page. Toggling emits `AppEvent::SetHookEnabled`, while trust actions emit `AppEvent::TrustHook` or `AppEvent::TrustHooks` with the current hash preserved.

#### Function details

##### `HooksBrowserView::new`  (lines 60–76)

```
fn new(
        hooks: Vec<HookMetadata>,
        warnings: Vec<String>,
        errors: Vec<codex_app_server_protocol::HookErrorInfo>,
        app_event_tx: AppEventSender,
    ) -> Self
```

**Purpose**: Test-only convenience constructor that wraps raw hook vectors and issue lists into a `HooksListEntry` and delegates to `from_entry` with the default list keymap.

**Data flow**: Consumes `hooks`, `warnings`, `errors`, and `app_event_tx` → builds a `HooksListEntry` with empty cwd, then calls `Self::from_entry(..., RuntimeKeymap::defaults().list)` → returns the initialized view.

**Call relations**: Used extensively by tests to avoid manually constructing `HooksListEntry` and keymaps.

*Call graph*: calls 1 internal fn (defaults); called by 18 (assert_unmanaged_toggle_key, managed_hooks_count_as_active, renders_command_details_with_three_line_cap, renders_empty_handler_browser_message, renders_event_browser_with_issues, renders_event_browser_with_review_column_when_needed, renders_review_needed_handler, renders_scrolled_handler_window, renders_selected_managed_handler, renders_untrusted_enabled_handler_as_inactive (+8 more)); 2 external calls (from_entry, new).


##### `HooksBrowserView::from_entry`  (lines 78–101)

```
fn from_entry(
        mut entry: HooksListEntry,
        app_event_tx: AppEventSender,
        keymap: ListKeymap,
    ) -> Self
```

**Purpose**: Constructs the hooks browser from a full `HooksListEntry`, sorting hooks and initializing the default event-page selection.

**Data flow**: Consumes mutable `entry`, `app_event_tx`, and `keymap` → sorts `entry.hooks` by `display_order`, initializes the view on `HooksBrowserPage::Events` with fresh `ScrollState` and `complete = false`, then if the page has rows sets `state.selected_idx` to the first event row whose aggregated `needs_review > 0`, or 0 if none → returns the view.

**Call relations**: Used by production code when opening the hooks browser and by the test-only `new` wrapper.

*Call graph*: calls 1 internal fn (new); called by 1 (open_hooks_browser).

**Data flow**: Starts from `event_header_lines()`, inserts a blank line, optionally appends a yellow `⚠ ...` review-needed message and another blank line, appends issue lines and spacing when present, then appends `event_table_lines()` → returns the full line vector.

### Modal entry and feedback views
This group contains the smaller bottom-pane text-entry and feedback-oriented modal views used for focused user prompts and note capture.

### `tui/src/bottom_pane/custom_prompt_view.rs`

`domain_logic` · `request handling`

This file defines `CustomPromptView`, a focused UI component for collecting a short custom prompt with optional context text. Its state includes static presentation fields (`title`, `placeholder`, `context_label`), a submission callback (`PromptSubmitted`), a `TextArea` plus `RefCell<TextAreaState>` for rendering/cursor tracking, a `PasteBurst` detector, and an optional `ViewCompletion` indicating whether the view has been accepted or cancelled.

Construction via `new` initializes an empty textarea or seeds it with `initial_text` and places the cursor at the end. Input handling is concentrated in `handle_key_event_at`, which takes an explicit `Instant` so tests can control paste-burst timing. Esc delegates to `on_ctrl_c`, marking the view cancelled. Enter has nuanced behavior: if `PasteBurst::direct_insert_newline_should_insert(now)` says the recent input looks like a paste burst, Enter inserts a literal newline and extends the burst window; otherwise plain Enter trims the textarea text and, if non-empty, invokes the submission callback and marks completion as `Accepted`. Modified Enter keys fall back to normal textarea input. Plain characters and Tab also cooperate with paste-burst detection so rapid pasted content does not accidentally submit after a short first line.

As a `BottomPaneView`, the component exposes completion state and explicit paste insertion. As a `Renderable`, it computes height from title/context/input/hint rows, draws a cyan `▌ ` gutter on each visible line, renders the textarea below the title and optional context line, overlays a dim placeholder when empty, clears the hint spacer row, and renders the standard popup hint line beneath the input. Cursor placement is derived from the textarea state and adjusted for the title/context/gutter layout.

#### Function details

##### `CustomPromptView::new`  (lines 46–69)

```
fn new(
        title: String,
        placeholder: String,
        initial_text: String,
        context_label: Option<String>,
        on_submit: PromptSubmitted,
    ) -> Self
```

**Purpose**: Constructs a custom-prompt view with title, placeholder, optional context label, initial text, and a submission callback.

**Data flow**: Takes owned `title`, `placeholder`, `initial_text`, optional `context_label`, and `on_submit`. It creates a new `TextArea`, seeds it with `initial_text` and moves the cursor to the end when non-empty, initializes `textarea_state` and `paste_burst` from defaults, sets `completion` to `None`, and returns the assembled `CustomPromptView`.

**Call relations**: Called by multiple bottom-pane flows that need a generic prompt editor, such as goal editing, rename prompts, marketplace add prompts, and custom review prompts.

*Call graph*: calls 1 internal fn (new); called by 5 (custom_prompt_view, show_goal_edit_prompt, show_rename_prompt, open_marketplace_add_prompt, show_review_custom_prompt); 3 external calls (new, default, default).


##### `CustomPromptView::handle_key_event_at`  (lines 71–125)

```
fn handle_key_event_at(&mut self, key_event: KeyEvent, now: Instant)
```

**Purpose**: Processes one key event for the prompt view using an explicit timestamp so paste-burst-sensitive Enter behavior can be tested deterministically.

**Data flow**: Reads `key_event`, `now`, textarea state, and paste-burst state. Esc calls `on_ctrl_c`. Enter first checks `paste_burst.direct_insert_newline_should_insert(now)`; if true it extends the burst window and inserts `"\n"`. Otherwise plain Enter trims `self.textarea.text()`, and if non-empty invokes `(self.on_submit)(text)` and sets `completion = Some(ViewCompletion::Accepted)`; modified Enter is passed to `textarea.input`. Plain non-control/non-alt chars and Tab, when paste bursts are allowed, update paste-burst tracking around `textarea.input`. All other keys go straight to `textarea.input` and clear explicit-paste state.

**Call relations**: This is the core input routine. The trait-level `handle_key_event` simply supplies `Instant::now()`, while tests call this method directly with controlled timestamps.

*Call graph*: calls 10 internal fn (on_ctrl_c, clear_after_explicit_paste, direct_insert_newline_should_insert, extend_window, on_plain_char_no_hold, allows_paste_burst, input, insert_str, text, has_ctrl_or_alt); called by 1 (handle_key_event).

```
fn handle_key_event(&mut self, key_event: KeyEvent)
```

**Purpose**: Trait adapter that handles a key event using the current wall-clock time.

**Data flow**: Takes a `KeyEvent`, calls `Instant::now()`, and forwards both to `handle_key_event_at`. It returns no value.

**Call relations**: Invoked through the `BottomPaneView` trait in normal runtime use.

*Call graph*: calls 1 internal fn (handle_key_event_at); 1 external calls (now).


**Purpose**: Inserts explicitly pasted text into the textarea and resets paste-burst state.

**Data flow**: Takes a `String` `pasted`; if empty returns `false`. Otherwise inserts the string into the textarea with `insert_str`, clears explicit-paste tracking in `paste_burst`, and returns `true`.

**Call relations**: Called by outer input plumbing for paste events that arrive as a full string rather than as rapid keypresses.

##### `CustomPromptView::render`  (lines 166–266)

```
fn render(&self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer)
```

**Purpose**: Draws the prompt view into a ratatui buffer, including title, optional context, guttered textarea, placeholder, and hint line.

**Data flow**: Takes `area` and `buf`; returns early for zero-sized areas. It computes `input_height`, renders the title line with `gutter()` and bold title text, optionally renders a cyan context line, then for each input row renders the gutter. It clears the top blank row of the input block, renders the textarea in the remaining rows using `StatefulWidgetRef::render_ref` with borrowed `textarea_state`, overlays a dim placeholder paragraph when the textarea is empty, clears the spacer row below the input, and renders `standard_popup_hint_line()` on the following row if within bounds.

**Call relations**: Called through the `Renderable` trait whenever this prompt view is visible in the bottom pane.

*Call graph*: calls 3 internal fn (input_height, standard_popup_hint_line, text); 4 external calls (from, new, render_ref, vec!).


##### `CustomPromptView::cursor_pos`  (lines 268–286)

```
fn cursor_pos(&self, area: Rect) -> Option<(u16, u16)>
```

**Purpose**: Computes the terminal cursor position inside the textarea portion of the rendered prompt view.

**Data flow**: Reads `area`; returns `None` if the area is too small for the guttered textarea. It computes `text_area_height` from `input_height(area.width) - 1`, adds one extra top row when `context_label` exists, constructs the textarea rectangle offset by the gutter and title/context rows, copies the current `TextAreaState` from the `RefCell`, and delegates to `self.textarea.cursor_pos_with_state(textarea_rect, state)`.

**Call relations**: Used by outer rendering/cursor management when this prompt view has focus.

*Call graph*: calls 2 internal fn (input_height, cursor_pos_with_state).


##### `CustomPromptView::input_height`  (lines 290–294)

```
fn input_height(&self, width: u16) -> u16
```

**Purpose**: Computes the height of the input block, including one blank top row plus the clamped textarea height.

**Data flow**: Subtracts the two-column gutter from `width` to get `usable_width`, asks the textarea for its desired height at that width, clamps that text height to `1..=8`, adds one extra row, caps the total at 9, and returns it.

**Call relations**: Shared by overall height calculation, rendering, and cursor-position computation so all three agree on the input block geometry.

*Call graph*: calls 1 internal fn (desired_height); called by 3 (cursor_pos, desired_height, render).


##### `gutter`  (lines 297–299)

```
fn gutter() -> Span<'static>
```

**Purpose**: Returns the styled gutter span used at the left edge of title, context, and input rows.

**Data flow**: Constructs and returns the static cyan span `"▌ "`.

**Call relations**: Used only by `render` to keep the prompt view's left gutter styling consistent.


### `tui/src/bottom_pane/feedback_view.rs`

`domain_logic` · `interactive popup handling and post-submit messaging`

This file has two main responsibilities. First, `FeedbackNoteView` is a minimal bottom-pane overlay that collects an optional freeform note before feedback submission. It stores the chosen `FeedbackCategory`, optional `turn_id`, whether logs should be included, an `AppEventSender`, a `TextArea`, a `RefCell<TextAreaState>` for cursor/render state, and a `complete` flag. Enter without modifiers submits; Esc cancels; modified Enter and all other keys are forwarded into the textarea. Submission trims whitespace, converts an empty note to `None`, emits `AppEvent::SubmitFeedback`, and closes.

Second, the file contains the surrounding feedback-flow helpers. `feedback_title_and_placeholder` and `feedback_classification` map categories to user-facing copy and stable identifiers. `feedback_selection_params`, `feedback_disabled_params`, and `feedback_upload_consent_params` build `SelectionViewParams` for the generic list popup system. The consent builder assembles a rich header listing exactly which attachments would be uploaded, conditionally including doctor reports, Windows sandbox logs, rollout files, auto-review rollout files, and diagnostics. It also appends connectivity diagnostics details only for non-`GoodResult` categories when diagnostics are present.

Finally, `feedback_success_cell` and `issue_url_for_category` generate post-upload history cells. The copy differs for OpenAI employees versus external users: employees get an internal go-link and a thread-sharing instruction, while external users get a GitHub issue URL prefilled with the uploaded thread ID. The tests in this file snapshot both rendering and exact emitted events/copy, making the messaging behavior intentionally strict.

#### Function details

##### `FeedbackNoteView::new`  (lines 61–76)

```
fn new(
        category: FeedbackCategory,
        turn_id: Option<String>,
        app_event_tx: AppEventSender,
        include_logs: bool,
    ) -> Self
```

**Purpose**: Creates the feedback note overlay with empty text input and default textarea state. It captures the feedback category, optional turn ID, event sender, and whether logs should be included in the eventual submission.

**Data flow**: Consumes `category`, `turn_id`, `app_event_tx`, and `include_logs` → initializes `TextArea::new()`, `RefCell<TextAreaState::default()>`, and `complete = false` → returns a ready-to-render `FeedbackNoteView`.

**Call relations**: Used when the app opens the note-entry step of the feedback flow and by tests that exercise rendering and submission behavior.

*Call graph*: calls 1 internal fn (new); called by 5 (feedback_view_with_connectivity_diagnostics, make_view, submit_feedback_emits_submit_event_with_trimmed_note, submit_feedback_omits_empty_note, show_feedback_note); 2 external calls (new, default).


##### `FeedbackNoteView::submit`  (lines 78–88)

```
fn submit(&mut self)
```

**Purpose**: Packages the current textarea contents into a feedback submission event and closes the view. It trims whitespace and omits the note entirely when the result is empty.

**Data flow**: Reads `self.textarea.text()`, trims it, converts non-empty text into `Some(String)` and empty text into `None`, clones `self.turn_id`, and reads `self.category` / `self.include_logs` → sends `AppEvent::SubmitFeedback { ... }` through `self.app_event_tx` and sets `self.complete = true`.

**Call relations**: Called from `handle_key_event` when the user presses plain Enter. It is the only path that emits the submit event.

*Call graph*: calls 2 internal fn (send, text); called by 1 (handle_key_event).


##### `FeedbackNoteView::handle_key_event`  (lines 92–116)

```
fn handle_key_event(&mut self, key_event: KeyEvent)
```

**Purpose**: Routes keyboard input between cancellation, submission, and normal textarea editing. It treats plain Enter specially as submit while still allowing modified Enter to be inserted into the text area.

**Data flow**: Consumes a `KeyEvent` → Esc calls `on_ctrl_c`; Enter with no modifiers calls `submit`; Enter with modifiers is forwarded to `self.textarea.input`; all other keys are also forwarded to `self.textarea.input` → mutates textarea contents/state and possibly completion.

**Call relations**: This is the `BottomPaneView` interaction entrypoint for the note overlay. It delegates either to `submit`, `on_ctrl_c`, or the embedded `TextArea`.

*Call graph*: calls 3 internal fn (on_ctrl_c, submit, input).


##### `FeedbackNoteView::on_ctrl_c`  (lines 118–121)


**Purpose**: Cancels the note-entry popup without sending feedback. It simply marks the view complete.

**Data flow**: Sets `self.complete = true` → returns `CancellationEvent::Handled`.


**Purpose**: Draws the intro title, guttered textarea, placeholder text, and standard popup hint. It also clears the placeholder/hint rows so stale buffer contents do not leak through.

**Data flow**: Reads `area`, intro lines, placeholder text from `feedback_title_and_placeholder`, and input height → early-returns on zero-sized areas; renders each intro line as a one-line `Paragraph`; draws a cyan gutter in the input area; clears the first editable row; renders the `TextArea` via `StatefulWidgetRef::render_ref`; overlays a dim placeholder when the textarea is empty; clears the blank row before the hint; then renders `standard_popup_hint_line()` if there is room → writes into `buf` and mutates only the borrowed textarea render state.

##### `FeedbackNoteView::input_height`  (lines 253–257)

```

```

**Purpose**: Determines the total vertical space reserved for the input section, including one gutter/header row above the actual textarea widget. The textarea itself is clamped to a compact multi-line range.

### Bottom-pane request and external-action overlays
These files implement the specialized overlays for app-link flows and server-driven elicitation or request-user-input interactions that sit above the persistent composer.

### `tui/src/bottom_pane/app_link_view.rs`

`domain_logic` · `request handling and bottom-pane interaction`

This file defines both the data model for app-link prompts and the interactive `AppLinkView` UI. `AppLinkViewParams` captures the app identity, descriptive text, URL, install/enable state, optional suggestion reason/type, and optional `AppLinkElicitationTarget` used to resolve MCP server elicitation requests. The `from_url_app_server_request` constructor is security-sensitive: it only accepts `McpServerElicitationRequest::Url`, validates HTTPS URLs with no embedded credentials, and for the special `codex_apps` server further restricts hosts to ChatGPT production/staging domains before extracting auth-failure metadata. Generic non-`codex_apps` URL requests become an `ExternalAction` prompt instead.

`AppLinkView` itself is a two-screen state machine: `Link` and `InstallConfirmation`. It tracks the selected action row, completion state, and a configurable `ListKeymap`. Action labels and behavior vary by suggestion type (`Install`, `Enable`, `Auth`, `ExternalAction`) and by whether the app is already installed or enabled. User actions emit `AppEvent`s such as `OpenUrlInBrowser`, `SetAppEnabled`, `RefreshConnectors`, and elicitation-resolution thread ops. Tool suggestions are special: declining or Ctrl-C resolves the elicitation as `Decline`, successful completion resolves it as `Accept`, and such views mark the terminal title as requiring action. Rendering uses ratatui paragraphs plus wrapped text, hyperlink marking, and a generic row renderer. The confirmation screen uses `adaptive_wrap_lines` so long URL tails remain visible even in narrow panes, which is a subtle but important usability choice covered by tests.

#### Function details

##### `AppLinkViewParams::from_url_app_server_request`  (lines 83–120)

```
fn from_url_app_server_request(
        thread_id: ThreadId,
        server_name: &str,
        request_id: AppServerRequestId,
        request: &codex_app_server_protocol::McpServerElicitationRequest
```

**Purpose**: Builds `AppLinkViewParams` from an MCP URL elicitation request when the request is trusted and supported. It distinguishes `codex_apps` auth flows from generic external-action flows.

**Data flow**: Takes `thread_id`, `server_name`, `request_id`, and a `McpServerElicitationRequest` reference → pattern-matches only the `Url` variant, otherwise returns `None` → validates the URL with `validate_external_url`, requiring ChatGPT hosts for `codex_apps` and only generic HTTPS trust checks otherwise → for `codex_apps` delegates to auth-metadata extraction, for other servers delegates to generic parameter construction → returns `Option<AppLinkViewParams>`.

**Call relations**: This is the main ingestion point used when the app decides whether a server request can be shown as an app-link view. It is invoked from request-routing code and delegates to the specialized constructors so the trust policy and request-shape checks happen before any UI is created.

*Call graph*: calls 1 internal fn (validate_external_url); called by 9 (interactive_request_for_thread_request, codex_apps_auth_url_elicitation_builds_auth_app_link_params, codex_apps_auth_url_elicitation_rejects_untrusted_urls, generic_url_elicitation_confirmation_snapshot, generic_url_elicitation_rejects_untrusted_urls, generic_url_elicitation_resolves_without_connector_refresh, generic_url_elicitation_snapshot, non_codex_apps_url_elicitation_builds_generic_app_link_params, handle_elicitation_request_now); 2 external calls (from_codex_apps_auth_url_parts, from_generic_url_parts).


##### `AppLinkViewParams::from_codex_apps_auth_url_parts`  (lines 122–176)

```
fn from_codex_apps_auth_url_parts(
        thread_id: ThreadId,
        server_name: &str,
        request_id: AppServerRequestId,
        meta: Option<&serde_json::Value>,
        message: &str,
```

**Purpose**: Extracts a ChatGPT app-authentication prompt from `codex_apps` metadata. It only succeeds for explicit connector auth-failure payloads.

**Data flow**: Consumes thread/request identity plus optional JSON `meta`, message text, trusted URL string, and elicitation id → walks nested JSON objects under `_codex_apps.connector_auth_failure` → requires `is_auth_failure == true` → derives `app_id` from `connector_id` or falls back to `elicitation_id`, derives `title` from `connector_name` or `app_id`, and fills fixed auth instructions and installed/enabled flags → returns `Some(AppLinkViewParams)` or `None` if any required metadata is absent or malformed.

**Call relations**: This helper is only reached from `AppLinkViewParams::from_url_app_server_request` for trusted `codex_apps` URLs. It encapsulates the metadata contract for auth failures so the caller can reject non-auth URL elicitation payloads cleanly.


##### `AppLinkViewParams::from_generic_url_parts`  (lines 178–203)

```
fn from_generic_url_parts(
        thread_id: ThreadId,
        server_name: &str,
        request_id: AppServerRequestId,
        message: &str,
        url: &str,
        elicitation_id: &str,
    )
```

**Purpose**: Creates a generic browser-action prompt for non-`codex_apps` URL elicitation requests. It labels the action as a generic required step rather than an app install/auth flow.

**Data flow**: Takes thread/request identity, server name, message, URL, and elicitation id → constructs an `AppLinkViewParams` with `app_id` from `elicitation_id`, title `"Action required"`, description `"Server: ..."`, fixed browser instructions, installed/enabled flags set true, suggestion reason from the message, suggestion type `ExternalAction`, and an `AppLinkElicitationTarget` → returns the populated struct.

**Call relations**: This is the non-auth branch of `AppLinkViewParams::from_url_app_server_request`. It exists so generic MCP servers can reuse the same UI while keeping their semantics distinct from ChatGPT app connector flows.

*Call graph*: 1 external calls (format!).


##### `validate_external_url`  (lines 206–218)

```
fn validate_external_url(url: &str, require_chatgpt_host: bool) -> Option<Url>
```

**Purpose**: Applies the trust policy for external URLs shown in the app-link view. It rejects non-HTTPS URLs, credential-bearing URLs, and optionally non-ChatGPT hosts.

**Data flow**: Accepts a raw URL string and a `require_chatgpt_host` flag → parses with `Url::parse` → rejects parse failures, non-`https` schemes, missing hosts, non-empty usernames, and any password → if ChatGPT host restriction is enabled, checks the host with `is_allowed_chatgpt_auth_host` → returns `Some(Url)` only for accepted URLs.

**Call relations**: Called only from `AppLinkViewParams::from_url_app_server_request` before any UI params are built. It centralizes the security gate so both auth and generic URL flows share the same baseline validation.

*Call graph*: calls 1 internal fn (is_allowed_chatgpt_auth_host); called by 1 (from_url_app_server_request); 1 external calls (parse).


##### `is_allowed_chatgpt_auth_host`  (lines 220–226)

```
fn is_allowed_chatgpt_auth_host(host: &str) -> bool
```

**Purpose**: Checks whether a hostname is an allowed ChatGPT auth domain for `codex_apps` browser flows. It accepts production and staging roots plus their subdomains.

**Data flow**: Takes a host string → lowercases it with `to_ascii_lowercase` → compares against exact roots `chatgpt.com` and `chatgpt-staging.com` and suffix matches for subdomains of those roots → returns a boolean.

**Call relations**: This helper is only used by `validate_external_url` when a `codex_apps` auth URL must be constrained to trusted ChatGPT hosts.

*Call graph*: called by 1 (validate_external_url).


##### `AppLinkView::new`  (lines 248–254)

```
fn new(params: AppLinkViewParams, app_event_tx: AppEventSender) -> Self
```

**Purpose**: Test-oriented convenience constructor that creates a view with the default runtime list keymap. It forwards to the configurable constructor.

**Data flow**: Takes `AppLinkViewParams` and an `AppEventSender` → fetches `RuntimeKeymap::defaults().list` → delegates to `new_with_keymap` → returns the initialized `AppLinkView`.

**Call relations**: Used heavily by tests and any code path that wants default list navigation behavior. It exists as a thin wrapper over `new_with_keymap` rather than duplicating initialization logic.

*Call graph*: calls 1 internal fn (defaults); called by 18 (auth_suggestion_with_reason_snapshot, declined_tool_suggestion_resolves_elicitation_decline, enable_suggestion_with_reason_snapshot, enable_tool_suggestion_resolves_elicitation_after_enable, generic_url_elicitation_confirmation_snapshot, generic_url_elicitation_resolves_without_connector_refresh, generic_url_elicitation_snapshot, horizontal_list_keys_move_action_selection, install_confirmation_does_not_split_long_url_like_token_without_scheme, install_confirmation_render_keeps_url_tail_visible_when_narrow (+8 more)); 1 external calls (new_with_keymap).


##### `AppLinkView::new_with_keymap`  (lines 256–290)

```
fn new_with_keymap(
        params: AppLinkViewParams,
        app_event_tx: AppEventSender,
        list_keymap: ListKeymap,
    ) -> Self
```

**Purpose**: Constructs the full `AppLinkView` state from parameters, event sender, and list key bindings. It initializes the view on the link screen with the first action selected.

**Data flow**: Consumes `AppLinkViewParams`, `AppEventSender`, and `ListKeymap` → destructures all parameter fields into the view struct → sets `screen` to `AppLinkScreen::Link`, `selected_action` to 0, `complete` to false, and stores the provided keymap and sender → returns the new view.

**Call relations**: This is the real constructor used by production code that opens app-link views and by tests that need custom keymaps. All later interaction methods assume the invariants established here.

*Call graph*: called by 3 (push_mcp_server_elicitation_request, remapped_horizontal_list_keys_control_action_selection, open_app_link_view).


##### `AppLinkView::action_labels`  (lines 292–324)

```
fn action_labels(&self) -> Vec<&'static str>
```

**Purpose**: Computes the visible action list for the current screen and suggestion type. The labels encode the view's state machine and are the source of truth for row count and numeric shortcuts.

**Data flow**: Reads `suggestion_type`, `elicitation_target`, `screen`, `is_installed`, and `is_enabled` → branches first on auth/external-action suggestion kinds, then on install/enable/default cases and current screen → returns a `Vec<&'static str>` of labels such as `Open sign-in URL`, `Disable app`, or `I finished`.

**Call relations**: Used by navigation, rendering, and numeric key handling. `move_selection_next`, `action_rows`, and `handle_key_event` all depend on this function so the current action set stays consistent with the active flow.

*Call graph*: calls 2 internal fn (is_auth_suggestion, is_external_action_suggestion); called by 3 (action_rows, handle_key_event, move_selection_next); 1 external calls (vec!).


##### `AppLinkView::move_selection_prev`  (lines 326–328)

```
fn move_selection_prev(&mut self)
```

**Purpose**: Moves the selected action one slot upward without underflowing below zero.

**Data flow**: Reads `selected_action` → applies `saturating_sub(1)` → writes the updated index back to `selected_action`.

**Call relations**: Invoked from keyboard handling for Up, BackTab, `k`, and remapped list-left bindings. It is a leaf state mutation used only for navigation.

*Call graph*: called by 1 (handle_key_event).


##### `AppLinkView::move_selection_next`  (lines 330–332)

```
fn move_selection_next(&mut self)
```

**Purpose**: Moves the selected action one slot downward, clamping at the last available action.

**Data flow**: Reads `selected_action` and the current action count from `action_labels()` → increments by one and clamps to `len - 1` → writes the result to `selected_action`.

**Call relations**: Invoked from keyboard handling for Down, Tab, `j`, and remapped list-right bindings. It depends on `action_labels` so selection bounds track the current screen and suggestion type.

*Call graph*: calls 1 internal fn (action_labels); called by 1 (handle_key_event).

##### `AppLinkView::resolve_elicitation`  (lines 351–363)

```
fn resolve_elicitation(&self, decision: McpServerElicitationAction)
```

**Purpose**: Sends an MCP elicitation resolution event for the current target, if one exists. It is the common path for accept/decline decisions.

**Data flow**: Reads `elicitation_target` → if absent, returns immediately → otherwise clones the target's server name and request id and calls `app_event_tx.resolve_elicitation` with the provided `McpServerElicitationAction` and `None` content/meta.

**Call relations**: This helper is called by decline, completion, Ctrl-C, and enable-toggle paths so all elicitation responses are emitted consistently.

*Call graph*: calls 1 internal fn (resolve_elicitation); called by 4 (complete_external_flow_and_close, decline_tool_suggestion, on_ctrl_c, toggle_enabled).

##### `AppLinkView::open_external_url`  (lines 370–378)

```
fn open_external_url(&mut self)
```

**Purpose**: Emits an event to open the configured URL in the browser and, when appropriate, advances to the confirmation screen.

**Data flow**: Clones `self.url` into `AppEvent::OpenUrlInBrowser` and sends it through `app_event_tx` → if the app is not installed or the suggestion is a browser-action suggestion, sets `screen = InstallConfirmation` and resets `selected_action` to 0.

**Call relations**: This is the primary action for install/auth/external-action flows and is reached from `activate_selected_action`. It delegates actual browser launching to the app event layer.

*Call graph*: calls 2 internal fn (send, is_browser_action_suggestion); called by 1 (activate_selected_action).


##### `AppLinkView::complete_external_flow_and_close`  (lines 380–394)

```
fn complete_external_flow_and_close(&mut self)
```

**Purpose**: Finalizes a browser-based flow, optionally refreshes connectors, resolves any tool suggestion as accepted, and marks the view complete.

**Data flow**: Checks whether connector refresh is needed: true when there is no elicitation target or when the target server is `codex_apps` → if so sends `AppEvent::RefreshConnectors { force_refetch: true }` → if this is a tool suggestion, resolves the elicitation as `Accept` → sets `complete = true`.

**Call relations**: Reached from `activate_selected_action` on confirmation-screen acceptance. It is the success path after the user returns from the browser.

*Call graph*: calls 3 internal fn (send, is_tool_suggestion, resolve_elicitation); called by 1 (activate_selected_action).


##### `AppLinkView::back_to_link_screen`  (lines 396–399)


**Purpose**: Returns from the confirmation screen to the original link screen and resets selection.

**Call relations**: Used only in non-tool flows where the user backs out of the confirmation screen instead of closing the whole view.

##### `AppLinkView::toggle_enabled`  (lines 401–411)

```
fn toggle_enabled(&mut self)
```

**Purpose**: Flips the app's enabled state, emits the corresponding app event, and for tool suggestions immediately resolves acceptance and closes.

**Data flow**: Negates `is_enabled` → sends `AppEvent::SetAppEnabled { id: app_id.clone(), enabled: is_enabled }` → if this is a tool suggestion, resolves the elicitation as `Accept` and sets `complete = true`.

**Call relations**: Called from `activate_selected_action` when the selected row is the enable/disable action. It bridges local UI state with the app-management backend.

*Call graph*: calls 3 internal fn (send, is_tool_suggestion, resolve_elicitation); called by 1 (activate_selected_action).


##### `AppLinkView::activate_selected_action`  (lines 413–472)

```
fn activate_selected_action(&mut self)
```

**Purpose**: Executes the currently selected action according to the current screen, install state, and suggestion type. This is the core state-machine dispatcher for the view.

**Data flow**: Reads `selected_action`, `screen`, `suggestion_type`, `is_installed`, and tool-suggestion status → for tool suggestions, branches by suggestion type and screen to call `open_external_url`, `toggle_enabled`, `complete_external_flow_and_close`, or `decline_tool_suggestion` → for non-tool flows, branches similarly but uses `complete = true` or `back_to_link_screen` instead of elicitation decline where appropriate.

**Call relations**: Invoked from `handle_key_event` on Enter or valid numeric shortcuts. It is the central control-flow node that delegates to all concrete action handlers.

*Call graph*: calls 6 internal fn (back_to_link_screen, complete_external_flow_and_close, decline_tool_suggestion, is_tool_suggestion, open_external_url, toggle_enabled); called by 1 (handle_key_event).

**Data flow**: Takes a width, computes booleans for auth/external-action and special `codex_apps` auth → pushes a bold heading, explanatory wrapped lines, a dim label (`Sign-in URL:`, `Link:`, or `Setup URL:`), and a cyan underlined URL line wrapped with `adaptive_wrap_lines` → returns the resulting lines.


**Purpose**: Processes keyboard input for navigation, numeric selection, activation, and cancellation.

**Data flow**: Matches the incoming `KeyEvent` → Esc routes to `on_ctrl_c`; Up/BackTab/plain `k` and remapped move-left bindings call `move_selection_prev`; Down/Tab/plain `j` and remapped move-right bindings call `move_selection_next`; digit keys map to zero-based action indices and, if in range, set `selected_action` then call `activate_selected_action`; Enter calls `activate_selected_action`; all other keys are ignored.

**Call relations**: This is the `BottomPaneView` input entrypoint for the view. It delegates all state changes to the smaller navigation and action methods.

*Call graph*: calls 5 internal fn (action_labels, activate_selected_action, move_selection_next, move_selection_prev, on_ctrl_c).

### `tui/src/bottom_pane/mcp_server_elicitation.rs`

`domain_logic` · `interactive app-server request handling`

This file has two layers. The first parses incoming `McpServerElicitationRequestParams` into a normalized `McpServerElicitationFormRequest`. It recognizes ordinary object schemas, boolean and enum/select fields, message-only schemas, tool-approval metadata, and tool-suggestion metadata. Message-only requests become approval-style select forms with `Allow`, optional persist variants, `Deny`, and/or `Cancel` depending on metadata. Tool approvals can also display a compact summary of selected tool parameters, preferring explicit display-order metadata and truncating long values.

The second layer is `McpServerElicitationOverlay`, the interactive popup. It owns the current request, a FIFO queue of later requests, a `ChatComposer` for text answers, per-field answer state (`ScrollState` for selects plus a `ComposerDraft` for text), the current field index, validation error text, completion flag, and list keymap. Select fields render as numbered `GenericDisplayRow`s; text fields reuse the composer, including pending-paste support and optional masking for secret fields. Drafts are saved and restored when moving between fields so partially entered answers persist.

Submission is field-aware. `field_value` only returns committed answers: select answers must be explicitly committed, and text answers must have been submitted through the composer. `submit_answers` first validates that all required fields are answered, jumping to the first missing field and surfacing a highlighted footer error if not. Otherwise it either resolves an approval action with optional persist metadata or sends a JSON object of field IDs to values as `content`. Esc always cancels immediately; Ctrl+C clears a non-empty text draft first, then cancels on a second invocation. The overlay also supports request queueing and external dismissal when the app server reports a request already resolved elsewhere.

#### Function details

##### `ComposerDraft::text_with_pending`  (lines 86–100)

```
fn text_with_pending(&self) -> String
```

**Purpose**: Returns the draft text with any pending paste placeholders expanded into their final textual form.

**Data flow**: Reads `self.text`, `self.text_elements`, and `self.pending_pastes` → if there are no pending pastes, clones and returns `self.text`; otherwise asserts that `text_elements` is non-empty, calls `ChatComposer::expand_pending_pastes`, and returns the expanded text string.

**Call relations**: Used when converting committed text answers into final submission values.

*Call graph*: calls 1 internal fn (expand_pending_pastes); 1 external calls (debug_assert!).

##### `McpServerElicitationFormRequest::from_app_server_request`  (lines 207–235)

```
fn from_app_server_request(
        thread_id: ThreadId,
        request_id: AppServerRequestId,
        request: McpServerElicitationRequestParams,
    ) -> Option<Self>
```

**Purpose**: Converts an app-server elicitation request into the normalized form-request model used by the overlay, but only for supported form requests.

**Data flow**: Consumes `thread_id`, `request_id`, and `request: McpServerElicitationRequestParams` → extracts `server_name` and the inner request, returns `None` unless it is `McpServerElicitationRequest::Form`, serializes `requested_schema` to `serde_json::Value`, and delegates to `Self::from_parts(...)`.

**Call relations**: Used when incoming app-server requests are turned into overlay requests and by tests that build normalized requests.

*Call graph*: called by 4 (interactive_request_for_thread_request, from_form_request, resolved_request_dismisses_overlay_without_emitting_events, handle_elicitation_request_now); 2 external calls (from_parts, to_value).


##### `McpServerElicitationFormRequest::from_parts`  (lines 237–354)

```
fn from_parts(
        thread_id: ThreadId,
        server_name: String,
        request_id: AppServerRequestId,
        meta: Option<Value>,
        message: String,
        requested_schema: Value,
```

**Purpose**: Builds a normalized elicitation form request from raw metadata, message text, and JSON schema, including approval-action fallbacks and tool-suggestion parsing.

**Data flow**: Consumes thread/server/request identifiers, optional `meta`, `message`, and `requested_schema` → parses optional tool suggestion metadata, detects tool-approval metadata and message-only schemas, optionally extracts approval display params, then chooses `(response_mode, fields)` as follows: tool suggestion plus message-only schema becomes an empty `FormContent` request; plain message-only schemas become a synthetic approval-action select field with options based on persist metadata and tool-approval status; otherwise it parses real fields from the schema via `parse_fields_from_schema`. It then returns the assembled `McpServerElicitationFormRequest`.

**Call relations**: Called only by `from_app_server_request`; it is the central normalization routine for all elicitation requests.

*Call graph*: calls 4 internal fn (approval_supports_persist_mode, parse_fields_from_schema, parse_tool_approval_display_params, parse_tool_suggestion_request); 5 external calls (String, as_object, is_null, new, vec!).

```
fn reset_for_request(&mut self)
```

**Purpose**: Reinitializes per-field answer state for the current request and clears composer content and validation errors.

**Data flow**: Reads `self.request.fields` → rebuilds `self.answers` so each select field gets a fresh `ScrollState` with its default selection and `answer_committed` reflecting whether a default exists, while each text field gets an empty `ComposerDraft` and `answer_committed = false`; resets `current_idx` to 0, clears `validation_error`, and clears the composer text content.

**Call relations**: Called when the overlay is first created and whenever it advances to the next queued request.

**Call relations**: Used by `render_prompt` to color unanswered prompts cyan.

*Call graph*: calls 2 internal fn (current_index, field_value); called by 1 (render_prompt).


##### `McpServerElicitationOverlay::option_index_for_digit`  (lines 1112–1119)

```
fn option_index_for_digit(&self, ch: char) -> Option<usize>
```

**Purpose**: Maps a numeric keypress to a zero-based option index for the current select field.

**Data flow**: Consumes `ch`, parses it as a digit, rejects 0, subtracts 1, and returns the index only when it is less than `options_len()`.

**Call relations**: Used by select-field key handling for one-key option submission.

*Call graph*: calls 1 internal fn (options_len); called by 1 (handle_key_event).


##### `McpServerElicitationOverlay::select_current_option`  (lines 1121–1127)

```
fn select_current_option(&mut self, committed: bool)
```

**Purpose**: Clamps the current select-field selection into bounds and optionally marks it committed.

**Data flow**: Reads `options_len()`, gets the mutable current answer, calls `answer.selection.clamp_selection(options_len)`, and sets `answer.answer_committed = committed`.

**Call relations**: Used by select-field key handling before advancing or submitting.

*Call graph*: calls 2 internal fn (current_answer_mut, options_len); called by 1 (handle_key_event).


##### `McpServerElicitationOverlay::clear_selection`  (lines 1129–1134)

```
fn clear_selection(&mut self)
```

**Purpose**: Clears the current select-field selection and marks the answer uncommitted.

*Call graph*: calls 1 internal fn (current_answer_mut); called by 1 (handle_key_event).


##### `McpServerElicitationOverlay::dispatch_cancel`  (lines 1136–1145)

```
fn dispatch_cancel(&self)
```

**Purpose**: Sends a cancellation resolution for the current elicitation request back to the app server.

**Data flow**: Reads the current request’s thread ID, server name, and request ID → calls `self.app_event_tx.resolve_elicitation(..., McpServerElicitationAction::Cancel, None, None)`.

**Call relations**: Used by Esc handling and final cancellation paths.

*Call graph*: calls 1 internal fn (resolve_elicitation); called by 2 (handle_key_event, on_ctrl_c).


##### `McpServerElicitationOverlay::advance_queue_or_complete`  (lines 1147–1155)

```
fn advance_queue_or_complete(&mut self)
```

**Purpose**: Moves to the next queued elicitation request if one exists, otherwise marks the overlay complete.

**Data flow**: Pops the front of `self.queue` → if a next request exists, replaces `self.request`, calls `reset_for_request()` and `restore_current_draft()`; otherwise sets `self.done = true`.

**Call relations**: Called after successful submission and when externally dismissing the current request.

*Call graph*: calls 2 internal fn (reset_for_request, restore_current_draft); called by 2 (dismiss_resolved_request, submit_answers); 1 external calls (pop_front).


##### `McpServerElicitationOverlay::submit_answers`  (lines 1157–1212)

```
fn submit_answers(&mut self)
```

**Purpose**: Validates required fields, resolves the current request back to the app server, and advances to the next queued request or completes the overlay.

**Data flow**: Saves the current draft, checks `first_required_unanswered_index()`, and on failure sets `validation_error`, jumps to that field, and returns. Otherwise clears the error and branches on `self.request.response_mode`: for `ApprovalAction`, maps the first field’s committed string value to `Accept`, `Decline`, or `Cancel` plus optional persist metadata and resolves the elicitation with no content; for `FormContent`, collects all committed field values into a `serde_json::Map` keyed by field ID, wraps it in `Value::Object`, resolves with `Accept`, and no meta. In both success cases it then calls `advance_queue_or_complete()`.

**Call relations**: Called by `go_next_or_submit` when the user reaches the last field or explicitly submits.

*Call graph*: calls 6 internal fn (resolve_elicitation, advance_queue_or_complete, field_value, first_required_unanswered_index, jump_to_field, save_current_draft); called by 1 (go_next_or_submit); 2 external calls (Object, json!).


##### `McpServerElicitationOverlay::dismiss_resolved_request`  (lines 1214–1233)

```
fn dismiss_resolved_request(&mut self, request: &ResolvedAppServerRequest) -> bool
```

**Purpose**: Removes a resolved elicitation request from either the active overlay or its queue without emitting any new events.

**Data flow**: Consumes `request: &ResolvedAppServerRequest`, returns false unless it is `ResolvedAppServerRequest::McpElicitation`, removes matching queued requests with `retain`, and if the active request matches calls `advance_queue_or_complete()` and returns true. Otherwise returns whether the queue length changed.

**Call relations**: Used by the bottom-pane trait method `dismiss_app_server_request` when the app server reports a request already resolved elsewhere.

*Call graph*: calls 1 internal fn (advance_queue_or_complete); called by 1 (dismiss_app_server_request); 2 external calls (len, retain).


##### `McpServerElicitationOverlay::go_next_or_submit`  (lines 1235–1241)

```
fn go_next_or_submit(&mut self)
```

**Purpose**: Advances to the next field when one remains, or submits the whole form when on the last field.

**Data flow**: Reads `current_index()` and `field_count()` → calls `submit_answers()` when the current field is the last one, otherwise `move_field(true)`.

**Call relations**: Used after committed answers from both select and text input paths.

*Call graph*: calls 4 internal fn (current_index, field_count, move_field, submit_answers); called by 2 (handle_composer_input_result, handle_key_event).


##### `McpServerElicitationOverlay::apply_submission_to_draft`  (lines 1243–1263)

```
fn apply_submission_to_draft(&mut self, text: String, text_elements: Vec<TextElement>)
```

**Purpose**: Stores a submitted text answer into the current answer state and mirrors it back into the composer as committed content.

**Data flow**: Consumes submitted `text` and `text_elements`, reads current local image paths from the composer, updates the mutable current answer’s `draft` with those values and empty `pending_pastes`, sets `answer_committed` based on whether trimmed text is non-empty, then updates the composer text content, moves the cursor to the end, and clears footer hints.

**Call relations**: Called by `handle_composer_input_result` when the embedded composer reports a submission or queued submission.

*Call graph*: calls 5 internal fn (local_images, move_cursor_to_end, set_footer_hint_override, set_text_content, current_answer_mut); called by 1 (handle_composer_input_result); 1 external calls (new).


##### `McpServerElicitationOverlay::handle_composer_input_result`  (lines 1265–1283)

```
fn handle_composer_input_result(&mut self, result: InputResult) -> bool
```

**Purpose**: Interprets the embedded composer’s input result and advances the elicitation flow when a text answer was submitted.

**Data flow**: Consumes `result: InputResult` → for `Submitted` and `Queued`, stores the answer via `apply_submission_to_draft`, clears `validation_error`, calls `go_next_or_submit()`, and returns true; for all other results returns false.

**Call relations**: Called from `handle_key_event` after forwarding a key event into the composer.

*Call graph*: calls 2 internal fn (apply_submission_to_draft, go_next_or_submit); called by 1 (handle_key_event).


**Purpose**: Renders the wrapped prompt text for the current field, coloring unanswered prompts cyan.

**Data flow**: Consumes `area` and `buf`, returns early on zero-sized areas, computes whether the current field is answered, iterates wrapped prompt lines, clips to the area height, styles each line cyan when unanswered, and renders each as a one-line `Paragraph`.

##### `McpServerElicitationOverlay::render_input`  (lines 1312–1334)

```
fn render_input(&self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer)
```

**Purpose**: Renders either the select-option list or the embedded composer for the current field.

**Data flow**: Consumes `area` and `buf`, returns early on zero-sized areas, then if the current field is select-style builds option rows, derives a selection state with a default selected row when needed, ensures visibility, and calls `render_rows`. Otherwise it renders the composer normally or masked with `*` when the field is secret.

**Call relations**: Called by `render` for the input region.

*Call graph*: calls 7 internal fn (render, render_with_mask, current_answer, current_field_is_secret, current_field_is_select, option_rows, render_rows); called by 1 (render).


##### `McpServerElicitationOverlay::render_footer`  (lines 1336–1385)

```
fn render_footer(&self, area: Rect, input_area_height: u16, buf: &mut Buffer)
```

**Purpose**: Renders wrapped footer tips, optionally prepending an `option X/Y` tip when not all select options fit in the visible input area.

**Data flow**: Consumes `area`, `input_area_height`, and `buf`, returns early on zero-sized areas, computes whether select options are hidden by comparing `options_required_height(area.width)` to `input_area_height`, optionally creates an `option {selected}/{total}` tip, merges it into the first wrapped footer-tip line from `footer_tip_lines(area.width)`, then for each visible footer row joins tips with `FOOTER_SEPARATOR`, styles highlighted tips cyan bold, dims the whole line, and renders it as a `Paragraph`.

**Call relations**: Called by `render` after drawing prompt and input.

*Call graph*: calls 6 internal fn (new, current_field_is_select, footer_tip_lines, options_len, options_required_height, selected_option_index); called by 1 (render); 6 external calls (from, new, new, format!, swap, vec!).


##### `McpServerElicitationOverlay::desired_height`  (lines 1389–1399)

```
fn desired_height(&self, width: u16) -> u16
```

**Purpose**: Computes the overlay’s desired height from wrapped prompt lines, input height, footer-tip lines, and menu-surface padding, with a minimum overlay height.

**Data flow**: Consumes `width`, constructs an outer rect, computes the inner rect with `menu_surface_inset`, measures wrapped prompt lines, input height, footer-tip line count, adds one progress row and menu-surface padding, and returns at least `MIN_OVERLAY_HEIGHT`.

**Call relations**: Used by layout code before rendering the overlay.

*Call graph*: calls 5 internal fn (footer_tip_lines, input_height, wrapped_prompt_lines, menu_surface_inset, menu_surface_padding_height); 1 external calls (new).


##### `McpServerElicitationOverlay::render`  (lines 1401–1473)

```
fn render(&self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer)
```

**Purpose**: Draws the full elicitation overlay, including progress line, wrapped prompt, input region, and footer tips inside the shared menu surface.

**Data flow**: Consumes `area` and `buf` → early-returns on zero-sized areas, paints the menu surface, computes wrapped prompt and footer lines, then allocates vertical space in priority order: one progress row, footer rows, minimum input height, prompt rows, and any remaining space back into the input area. It renders a progress line showing `Field i/n` and required-unanswered count, then calls `render_prompt`, `render_input`, and `render_footer` with the computed sub-areas.

**Call relations**: Main rendering implementation for the overlay, used by the TUI and snapshot tests.

*Call graph*: calls 10 internal fn (current_field_is_select, current_index, field_count, footer_tip_lines, render_footer, render_input, render_prompt, required_unanswered_count, wrapped_prompt_lines, render_menu_surface); called by 1 (render_snapshot); 4 external calls (from, new, format!, from).

##### `McpServerElicitationOverlay::handle_key_event`  (lines 1513–1643)

```
fn handle_key_event(&mut self, key_event: KeyEvent)
```

**Purpose**: Implements all keyboard interaction for the elicitation overlay, including field navigation, select-option movement/commit, text-composer forwarding, and immediate Esc cancellation.

**Data flow**: Consumes a `KeyEvent` → ignores release events; Esc dispatches cancel and marks the overlay done; Ctrl+P/PageUp and Ctrl+N/PageDown move between fields; on select fields, left/right and configured list-keymap horizontal bindings also move between fields. If the current field is select-style, it clears validation errors and handles Up/Down or j/k movement, Backspace/Delete clearing, space committing the current option, Enter committing and advancing/submitting, and digit shortcuts that select, commit, and immediately advance. For text fields, it snapshots the composer draft before and after forwarding the key event into `self.composer.handle_key_event`, handles submitted results via `handle_composer_input_result`, and if the draft changed clears validation errors and marks the current answer uncommitted.

**Call relations**: This is the main `BottomPaneView` interaction entrypoint for elicitation requests. It orchestrates both select-style and text-style field behavior.

*Call graph*: calls 13 internal fn (handle_key_event, capture_composer_draft, clear_selection, current_answer_mut, current_field_is_select, dispatch_cancel, go_next_or_submit, handle_composer_input_result, move_field, option_index_for_digit (+3 more)); 1 external calls (matches!).

*Call graph*: calls 4 internal fn (current_text_with_pending, clear_current_draft, current_field_is_select, dispatch_cancel).
