### `tui/src/bottom_pane/request_user_input/render.rs`

`domain_logic` · `request handling`

This file is the presentation layer for `RequestUserInputOverlay`. It implements `Renderable` and contains the concrete layout math and drawing code for two distinct UI modes: the standard question-answer overlay and a confirmation dialog shown when unanswered questions remain. The standard path paints the shared menu surface, lays out progress, wrapped question text, selectable option rows, an optional notes composer, and footer tips. It colors unanswered questions cyan, appends an auto-resolution countdown in red when present, bottom-aligns option rows so footer spacing stays visually stable, and truncates footer hint lines at word boundaries with a styled ellipsis.

The unanswered-confirmation path builds owned `Line<'static>` values for title, subtitle, hint text, and selection rows, wraps header and hint lines to the available width, measures row height with the shared popup helpers, and renders header → spacer → rows → spacer → hints inside the same menu surface treatment used elsewhere.

Several invariants are enforced defensively: zero-sized areas short-circuit immediately; widths are clamped to at least one cell before wrapping or measuring; cursor reporting is disabled unless notes are focused and visible; and secret questions render the notes composer with `*` masking. The helper `render_rows_bottom_aligned` uses a scratch buffer to reuse the shared row renderer while shifting the rendered block downward, and `truncate_line_word_boundary_with_ellipsis` preserves span styling while scanning Unicode display widths character-by-character.

#### Function details

##### `line_to_owned`  (lines 47–60)

```
fn line_to_owned(line: Line<'_>) -> Line<'static>
```

**Purpose**: Converts a borrowed `Line` into an owned `Line<'static>` while preserving line-level style, alignment, and each span’s style. It is used when wrapped lines need to outlive the borrowed source text.

**Data flow**: Takes a `Line<'_>` by value, reads its `style`, `alignment`, and `spans`, converts each span’s `content` into owned `Cow::Owned(String)`, and returns a new `Line<'static>` with the same visual attributes.

**Call relations**: This is a local helper used by the unanswered-confirmation layout path after `wrap_styled_line` returns borrowed lines; it exists so later rendering can move those wrapped lines around without lifetime coupling to the original source.


##### `RequestUserInputOverlay::desired_height`  (lines 63–106)

```
fn desired_height(&self, width: u16) -> u16
```

**Purpose**: Computes the total vertical space the overlay wants for the current width, accounting for menu padding, progress row, wrapped question text, optional options, optional notes composer, footer hints, and the alternate unanswered-confirmation mode.

**Data flow**: Reads overlay state such as `confirm_unanswered_active`, `has_options`, `notes_ui_visible`, wrapped question lines, option and notes heights, and footer height. It transforms the available width through `menu_surface_inset` to get inner content width, sums the relevant section heights plus `PROGRESS_ROW_HEIGHT` and `menu_surface_padding_height`, applies different spacer rules depending on whether notes are visible, and returns at least `MIN_OVERLAY_HEIGHT` as `u16`.

**Call relations**: This is invoked by outer layout code through the `Renderable` trait before rendering. When the confirmation dialog is active it delegates entirely to `RequestUserInputOverlay::unanswered_confirmation_height`; otherwise it performs the standard overlay sizing logic itself.

*Call graph*: calls 3 internal fn (unanswered_confirmation_height, menu_surface_inset, menu_surface_padding_height); 1 external calls (new).


##### `RequestUserInputOverlay::render`  (lines 108–110)

```
fn render(&self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer)
```

**Purpose**: Entry point for drawing the overlay through the `Renderable` trait. It renders using the current instant so countdown text can reflect wall-clock time.

**Data flow**: Receives the target `Rect` and mutable `Buffer`, obtains `Instant::now()`, and forwards all work to `render_ui_at`. It writes only through the delegated renderer.

**Call relations**: This is called by the TUI rendering pipeline whenever the overlay is visible. It delegates immediately to `RequestUserInputOverlay::render_ui_at`, supplying the current time needed for countdown display.

*Call graph*: calls 1 internal fn (render_ui_at); 1 external calls (now).


##### `RequestUserInputOverlay::cursor_pos`  (lines 112–114)

```
fn cursor_pos(&self, area: Rect) -> Option<(u16, u16)>
```

**Purpose**: Reports the terminal cursor position for the overlay when the notes composer should own the cursor. It is the `Renderable` trait hook used by the outer UI.

**Data flow**: Accepts the overlay area, forwards it to `cursor_pos_impl`, and returns either `Some((x, y))` for the notes input cursor or `None` when no cursor should be shown.

**Call relations**: The rendering framework calls this after layout. The method is just a trait adapter around `RequestUserInputOverlay::cursor_pos_impl`.

*Call graph*: calls 1 internal fn (cursor_pos_impl).


##### `RequestUserInputOverlay::unanswered_confirmation_data`  (lines 118–131)

```
fn unanswered_confirmation_data(&self) -> UnansweredConfirmationData
```

**Purpose**: Builds the raw content model for the unanswered-confirmation popup: title, subtitle with pluralization, shared hint line, selectable rows, and scroll state.

**Data flow**: Reads `unanswered_question_count`, formats a subtitle string with singular/plural handling, fetches the standard popup hint line, obtains confirmation rows from overlay state, and copies `confirm_unanswered` scroll state with a default fallback. It returns an `UnansweredConfirmationData` struct.

**Call relations**: This is only used by `RequestUserInputOverlay::unanswered_confirmation_layout`, which turns the raw lines into wrapped owned lines suitable for measurement and rendering.

*Call graph*: calls 1 internal fn (standard_popup_hint_line); called by 1 (unanswered_confirmation_layout); 2 external calls (from, format!).


##### `RequestUserInputOverlay::unanswered_confirmation_layout`  (lines 133–150)

```
fn unanswered_confirmation_layout(&self, width: u16) -> UnansweredConfirmationLayout
```

**Purpose**: Transforms unanswered-confirmation content into a width-specific layout with wrapped owned header and hint lines plus the row list and scroll state.

**Data flow**: Takes a content width, calls `unanswered_confirmation_data`, wraps the title, subtitle, and hint lines with `wrap_styled_line`, converts those wrapped lines to owned `'static` lines via `line_to_owned`, and returns an `UnansweredConfirmationLayout` containing wrapped headers, wrapped hints, rows, and state.

**Call relations**: Both `RequestUserInputOverlay::unanswered_confirmation_height` and `RequestUserInputOverlay::render_unanswered_confirmation` depend on this helper so measurement and rendering use the same wrapped content.

*Call graph*: calls 2 internal fn (unanswered_confirmation_data, wrap_styled_line); called by 2 (render_unanswered_confirmation, unanswered_confirmation_height).


##### `RequestUserInputOverlay::unanswered_confirmation_height`  (lines 152–170)

```
fn unanswered_confirmation_height(&self, width: u16) -> u16
```

**Purpose**: Measures how tall the unanswered-confirmation popup must be at a given width, including wrapped header lines, wrapped hint lines, row height, spacer rows, and menu padding.

**Data flow**: Builds an outer `Rect`, shrinks it with `menu_surface_inset`, computes inner width, derives a wrapped layout with `unanswered_confirmation_layout`, measures row height with `measure_rows_height`, adds one spacer after the header and one before hints, adds `menu_surface_padding_height`, and returns at least `MIN_OVERLAY_HEIGHT`.

**Call relations**: This function is selected by `RequestUserInputOverlay::desired_height` whenever the confirmation mode is active, ensuring the outer layout reserves enough space before `render_unanswered_confirmation` draws.

*Call graph*: calls 4 internal fn (unanswered_confirmation_layout, measure_rows_height, menu_surface_inset, menu_surface_padding_height); called by 1 (desired_height); 1 external calls (new).


##### `RequestUserInputOverlay::render_unanswered_confirmation`  (lines 172–246)

```
fn render_unanswered_confirmation(&self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer)
```

**Purpose**: Draws the unanswered-confirmation popup inside the shared menu surface, rendering wrapped header lines, a scrollable row list, and wrapped hint lines with spacing between sections.

**Data flow**: Receives the outer area and buffer, paints the menu surface via `render_menu_surface`, aborts on empty content area, computes a wrapped layout for the content width, renders header lines one row at a time, reserves a blank spacer if space remains, computes remaining height, splits that into rows area and optional spacer-before-hint, renders rows with `render_rows`, then renders hint lines until vertical space is exhausted.

**Call relations**: This path is chosen by `RequestUserInputOverlay::render_ui_at` when `confirm_unanswered_active()` is true. It reuses the shared row renderer so confirmation choices behave like other selection popups.

*Call graph*: calls 3 internal fn (unanswered_confirmation_layout, render_menu_surface, render_rows); called by 1 (render_ui_at); 2 external calls (new, from).


##### `RequestUserInputOverlay::render_ui_at`  (lines 248–380)

```
fn render_ui_at(&self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer, now: Instant)
```

**Purpose**: Implements the full standard overlay rendering pipeline, including progress header, question prompt, options list, notes composer, and footer tips, while also dispatching to the unanswered-confirmation renderer when needed.

**Data flow**: Reads the target area, current time, and extensive overlay state: confirmation mode, section layout, notes visibility, progress prefix, auto-resolution countdown, current answer/options state, selected option index, current composer text, and footer tips. It paints the menu surface, renders progress text, colors question lines cyan until answered, bottom-aligns option rows after ensuring the selected option is visible, renders masked or plain notes input when shown, computes whether options are clipped to synthesize an `option {selected}/{total}` footer tip, builds footer tip lines, truncates each line with `truncate_line_word_boundary_with_ellipsis`, and writes all output into the buffer.

**Call relations**: This is the main renderer called by `RequestUserInputOverlay::render`. It branches first to `render_unanswered_confirmation` for the alternate mode; otherwise it orchestrates the standard overlay and delegates row drawing to `render_rows_bottom_aligned`, notes drawing to `render_notes_input`, and footer truncation to `truncate_line_word_boundary_with_ellipsis`.

*Call graph*: calls 6 internal fn (new, render_notes_input, render_unanswered_confirmation, render_rows_bottom_aligned, truncate_line_word_boundary_with_ellipsis, render_menu_surface); called by 1 (render); 5 external calls (from, new, new, format!, vec!).


##### `RequestUserInputOverlay::cursor_pos_impl`  (lines 383–406)

```
fn cursor_pos_impl(&self, area: Rect) -> Option<(u16, u16)>
```

**Purpose**: Determines whether the notes composer should expose a cursor and, if so, computes its absolute terminal coordinates within the overlay.

**Data flow**: Reads overlay mode and focus state (`confirm_unanswered_active`, `has_options`, `notes_ui_visible`, `focus_is_notes`), computes the inset content area with `menu_surface_inset`, derives section layout, extracts `notes_area`, and asks `self.composer.cursor_pos(input_area)` for the final coordinates. It returns `None` whenever notes are hidden, unfocused, or the area is empty.

**Call relations**: This is the implementation behind the `Renderable` cursor hook `RequestUserInputOverlay::cursor_pos`. It mirrors the same visibility rules used by `render_ui_at` so the cursor only appears when the notes editor is actually on screen.

*Call graph*: calls 1 internal fn (menu_surface_inset); called by 1 (cursor_pos).


##### `RequestUserInputOverlay::render_notes_input`  (lines 409–421)

```
fn render_notes_input(&self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer)
```

**Purpose**: Renders the notes composer, masking characters for secret questions and using normal composer rendering otherwise.

**Data flow**: Takes the notes area and buffer, returns early for zero-sized areas, reads `current_question().is_secret`, and then calls either `composer.render_with_mask(area, buf, Some('*'))` or `composer.render(area, buf)` to write the input UI.

**Call relations**: This helper is called only from `RequestUserInputOverlay::render_ui_at` after section layout decides that notes are visible and have nonzero height.

*Call graph*: called by 1 (render_ui_at).


##### `line_width`  (lines 424–428)

```
fn line_width(line: &Line<'_>) -> usize
```

**Purpose**: Computes the terminal display width of a styled `Line` by summing the Unicode display widths of all span contents.

**Data flow**: Iterates over the line’s spans, reads each span’s string content, measures it with `UnicodeWidthStr::width`, sums the widths, and returns the total as `usize`.

**Call relations**: This helper is used by `truncate_line_word_boundary_with_ellipsis` as the fast path to detect when a line already fits and no truncation is needed.

*Call graph*: called by 1 (truncate_line_word_boundary_with_ellipsis); 1 external calls (iter).


##### `render_rows_bottom_aligned`  (lines 435–470)

```
fn render_rows_bottom_aligned(
    area: Rect,
    buf: &mut Buffer,
    rows: &[crate::bottom_pane::selection_popup_common::GenericDisplayRow],
    state: &ScrollState,
    max_results: usize,
    em
```

**Purpose**: Renders selection rows into a region while anchoring the visible block to the bottom of that region when fewer lines are produced than the region’s height.

**Data flow**: Accepts a target area, buffer, row slice, scroll state, result limit, and empty-message text. It copies the destination area into a scratch `Buffer`, calls the shared `render_rows` into that scratch area, computes the rendered height and bottom offset, then copies only the visible rendered lines back into the original buffer shifted downward.

**Call relations**: This helper is used by `RequestUserInputOverlay::render_ui_at` for the options list. It exists because the shared row renderer top-aligns output, but this overlay wants options visually anchored above the footer.

*Call graph*: calls 1 internal fn (render_rows); called by 1 (render_ui_at); 2 external calls (empty, new).


##### `truncate_line_word_boundary_with_ellipsis`  (lines 479–578)

```
fn truncate_line_word_boundary_with_ellipsis(
    line: Line<'static>,
    max_width: usize,
) -> Line<'static>
```

**Purpose**: Truncates a styled line to a maximum display width, preferring to cut at whitespace, trimming trailing spaces, and appending a style-preserving ellipsis.

**Data flow**: Consumes a `Line<'static>` and `max_width`. It first returns an empty line for width 0 or the original line if `line_width` says it fits. Otherwise it reserves space for `…`, scans spans character-by-character using Unicode display widths, tracks the last fitting position and last whitespace breakpoint, chooses the preferred cut point, rebuilds spans up to that byte boundary, trims trailing whitespace-only spans, styles the ellipsis to match the last visible span or line style, appends it, and returns the truncated line.

**Call relations**: This helper is called from `RequestUserInputOverlay::render_ui_at` when rendering footer hint lines, where preserving styling and avoiding awkward mid-word cuts matters for compact bottom-pane hints.

*Call graph*: calls 1 internal fn (line_width); called by 1 (render_ui_at); 6 external calls (from, styled, width, width, new, new).
